response.justification: Bogdan has an impressive and broad software engineering career spanning 15+ years, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, he has no Japanese language proficiency — a stated mandatory requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate. His primary expertise is in web and application development (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, DLang), with only peripheral C/C++ exposure and no evidence of embedded networking, L2/L3 switch development, security protocol implementation (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal), or Linux kernel-level source modification work.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience including some embedded systems exposure (robotic arms, hardware control at Altom) and C/C++ usage at National Instruments and in personal projects
Strong Linux familiarity and infrastructure experience (CI/CD, Docker, GitLab, dev-ops tooling) provides a partial foundation
Demonstrated systems-level thinking through low-latency async I/O work at Sociomantic and hardware-adjacent projects
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a hard mandatory requirement; candidate speaks English, Romanian, and basic German only
No experience with embedded networking security protocols (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, MAC authentication, Captive Portal) or network switch architecture
Primary technical stack (TypeScript/Node.js/web frameworks) is almost entirely irrelevant to embedded C firmware development for LAN-switch security features

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or switch security experience
